symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly spreading thick fissuring/bleeding plaques with fatigue

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe plaque psoriasis flare with systemic burden
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Extensive fissuring plaques with systemic fatigue on a known background is a severe plaque flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: No sterile pustules/pus lakes; no high fever/rigors

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of widespread sterile pustules and high fever argues against generalized pustular psoriasis, a dermatological emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: No spreading unilateral hot area/honey crust/punched-out vesicles

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse symmetric erythema without a unilateral spreading hot area argues against cellulitis.
Absence of punched-out vesicles argues against eczema herpeticum.
No shivering/temperature or haemodynamic instability lowers the probability of decompensated erythroderma, though extensive disease still warrants review.

symptoms.symptom_name: Flare after stopping topical steroid + stress

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe plaque psoriasis flare with systemic burden
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear trigger of medication cessation/stress supports a flare that should respond to resuming topical therapy.

symptoms.symptom_name: ~35% BSA plaques with fissures, no fluctuance/honey crust/streaking, no synovitis (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe plaque psoriasis flare with systemic burden
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Extensive plaques without secondary-infection signs confirm a severe plaque flare in person.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Severe plaque psoriasis flare with systemic burden
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: An extensive fissuring plaque flare with systemic fatigue, no pustules/high fever, no spreading hot area/punched-out vesicles, and no systemic instability, precipitated by stopping topical steroids, is a severe plaque flare — best managed by restarting topical therapy plus emollients/cooling and prompt in-person/dermatology review, with emergency safety-netting for pustular psoriasis, erythroderma instability and secondary sepsis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To assess extent, exclude pustular/erythrodermic transformation and secondary infection, and plan systemic therapy; cannot be done fully via chat.
To assess for infection/erythroderma complications if red flags appear.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Skin swab/biopsy only if secondary infection/atypical features
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not a first remote step for a typical plaque flare.
